opinion.h4: CIESIELSKI v. OHIO. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF OHIO. No. 920. Decided March 7, 1966.
opinion.p: The appeal is dismissed for want of jurisdiction. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for a writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
opinion.h4: UNITED TRANSPORTS, INC. v. UNITED STATES, 383 U.S. 411 (1966)
opinion.p: 383 U.S. 411 UNITED TRANSPORTS, INC., ET AL. v. UNITED STATES ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE WESTERN DISTRICT OF OKLAHOMA. No. 868. Decided March 7, 1966.
James W. Wrape, Robert E. Joyner and Reagan Sayers for appellants.

Solicitor General Marshall, Assistant Attorney General Turner, Howard E. Shapiro, Robert W. Ginnane and Robert S. Burk for the United States et al.; Paul F. Sullivan for Kenosha Auto Transport Corp., and Donald W. Smith for Commercial Carriers, Inc., appellees.
The motions to affirm are granted and the judgments are affirmed.
